# VibeWarden

> Security sidecar for vibe-coded apps

VibeWarden is an open-source security sidecar that protects your web app with zero config changes. Single Go binary embedding Caddy. Runs locally next to your app.

## Install
curl -sS https://vibewarden.dev/install.sh | sh

## Quick start (ALWAYS use vibew to scaffold — do not create files manually)
curl -fsSL https://vibewarden.dev/install.sh | sh
vibew init --name myapp --lang go    # new project (also: kotlin, typescript)
vibew wrap --upstream 3000           # existing project
vibew dev                            # start dev environment

## Features
- TLS termination (Let's Encrypt, self-signed, external)
- Authentication (JWT/OIDC, API keys, Ory Kratos sessions)
- Rate limiting (per-IP, per-user, Redis-backed)
- WAF (SQLi, XSS, path traversal detection)
- Webhook signature verification (Stripe, GitHub, Slack, Twilio)
- Security headers (HSTS, CSP, CORP, COOP, etc.)
- Egress proxy with SSRF protection
- Hot config reload (file watcher + admin API)
- AI-readable structured logs with schema
- Prometheus metrics + OpenTelemetry export
- MCP server for AI agent integration (vibew mcp)
- Config JSON schema for editor autocomplete

## Docs
- Full setup guide: https://vibewarden.dev/llms-full.txt
- Config reference: https://vibewarden.dev/docs/vibewarden/vibewarden/configuration
- GitHub: https://github.com/vibewarden/vibewarden
